pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 93-104: Directory created with mkdir -p and file created with touch, populated with patient names from target list

setup_task.sh lines 29-42: CREATE TABLE ArchivedPatients with fields GUID, Nom, Prenom, DateNaissance, Sexe, Adresse, CodePostal, Ville, Tel, NumSS. Referenced throughout setup_task.sh and export_result.sh
setup_task.sh line 23 (DB_NAME='DrTuxTest'), setup_medintux.sh creates database with 'CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS DrTuxTest', referenced in all scripts including task_utils.sh line 19

privileged_info_summary: The task 'reactivate_archived_records' uses synthetic data with three target patients generated on-the-fly during setup. The request file at /home/ga/Documents/restore_requests.txt contains patient names to restore. Data is stored in the 'ArchivedPatients' table in the 'DrTuxTest' MySQL database. The task requires restoring patient records from the ArchivedPatients table to active tables (fchpat for demographics and IndexNomPrenom for search index) and removing them from the archive. Data generation uses random French names (MARTIN Sophie, BERNARD Michel, THOMAS David, etc.) and synthetic social security numbers, dates of birth, and addresses.
